question: For the `Book` class (from Q1), overload the binary `==` operator to compare two `Book` objects. Two books should be considered equal if their `isbn` values are identical.

test_cases.input: [{"title": "The Hobbit", "author": "J.R.R. Tolkien", "isbn": "978-0-345-33968-3"}, {"title": "The Lord of the Rings", "author": "J.R.R. Tolkien", "isbn": "978-0-345-33968-3"}]

driver: std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<Book, Book>, bool>> testCases = { /* ... */ }; for (const auto& tc : testCases) { bool result = (tc.first.first == tc.first.second); /* ... */ }
errors: The overloaded '==' operator compares the 'title' of the books instead of their 'isbn' values. According to the problem statement, books should be considered equal if their 'isbn' values are identical, leading to incorrect equality checks for cases where titles differ but ISBNs are the same, or vice versa.
